class.name: DefaultMutableTreeNode
class.signature: public class DefaultMutableTreeNode extends Object implements Cloneable, MutableTreeNode, Serializable
class.description: A DefaultMutableTreeNode is a general-purpose node in a tree data structure. For examples of using default mutable tree nodes, see How to Use Trees in The Java Tutorial. A tree node may have at most one parent and 0 or more children. DefaultMutableTreeNode provides operations for examining and modifying a node's parent and children and also operations for examining the tree that the node is a part of. A node's tree is the set of all nodes that can be reached by starting at the node and following all the possible links to parents and children. A node with no parent is the root of its tree; a node with no children is a leaf. A tree may consist of many subtrees, each node acting as the root for its own subtree. This class provides enumerations for efficiently traversing a tree or subtree in various orders or for following the path between two nodes. A DefaultMutableTreeNode may also hold a reference to a user object, the use of which is left to the user. Asking a DefaultMutableTreeNode for its string representation with toString() returns the string representation of its user object. This is not a thread safe class.If you intend to use a DefaultMutableTreeNode (or a tree of TreeNodes) in more than one thread, you need to do your own synchronizing. A good convention to adopt is synchronizing on the root node of a tree. While DefaultMutableTreeNode implements the MutableTreeNode interface and will allow you to add in any implementation of MutableTreeNode not all of the methods in DefaultMutableTreeNode will be applicable to all MutableTreeNodes implementations. Especially with some of the enumerations that are provided, using some of these methods assumes the DefaultMutableTreeNode contains only DefaultMutableNode instances. All of the TreeNode/MutableTreeNode methods will behave as defined no matter what implementations are added. Warning: Serialized objects of this class will not be compatible with future Swing releases. The current serialization support is appropriate for short term storage or RMI between applications running the same version of Swing. As of 1.4, support for long term storage of all JavaBeans has been added to the java.beans package. Please see XMLEncoder.

constructors.name: DefaultMutableTreeNode
constructors.description: Creates a tree node that has no parent and no children, but which allows children.

constructors.name: DefaultMutableTreeNode
constructors.description: Creates a tree node with no parent, no children, but which allows children, and initializes it with the specified user object.

parameters.name: userObject
parameters.description: an Object provided by the user that constitutes the node's data

constructors.name: DefaultMutableTreeNode
constructors.description: Creates a tree node with no parent, no children, initialized with the specified user object, and that allows children only if specified.

parameters.name: userObject
parameters.description: an Object provided by the user that constitutes the node's data

parameters.name: allowsChildren
parameters.description: if true, the node is allowed to have child nodes -- otherwise, it is always a leaf node

methods.name: insert
methods.signature: public void insert(MutableTreeNode newChild, int childIndex)
methods.description: Removes newChild from its present parent (if it has a parent), sets the child's parent to this node, and then adds the child to this node's child array at index childIndex. newChild must not be null and must not be an ancestor of this node.

parameters.name: newChild
parameters.description: the MutableTreeNode to insert under this node

parameters.name: childIndex
parameters.description: the index in this node's child array where this node is to be inserted

throws.description: if childIndex is out of bounds
if newChild is null or is an ancestor of this node
if this node does not allow children

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes the child at the specified index from this node's children and sets that node's parent to null. The child node to remove must be a MutableTreeNode.

parameters.name: childIndex
parameters.description: the index in this node's child array of the child to remove

throws.description: if childIndex is out of bounds

methods.name: setParent
methods.description: Sets this node's parent to newParent but does not change the parent's child array. This method is called from insert() and remove() to reassign a child's parent, it should not be messaged from anywhere else.

parameters.name: newParent
parameters.description: this node's new parent

methods.name: getParent
methods.description: Returns this node's parent or null if this node has no parent.
methods.returns: this node's parent TreeNode, or null if this node has no parent

methods.name: getChildAt
methods.description: Returns the child at the specified index in this node's child array.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: an index into this node's child array

throws.description: if index is out of bounds

methods.returns: the TreeNode in this node's child array at the specified index

methods.name: getChildCount
methods.description: Returns the number of children of this node.
methods.returns: an int giving the number of children of this node

methods.name: getIndex
methods.description: Returns the index of the specified child in this node's child array. If the specified node is not a child of this node, returns -1. This method performs a linear search and is O(n) where n is the number of children.

parameters.name: aChild
parameters.description: the TreeNode to search for among this node's children

methods.returns: an int giving the index of the node in this node's child array, or -1 if the specified node is a not a child of this node

methods.name: children
methods.description: Creates and returns a forward-order enumeration of this node's children. Modifying this node's child array invalidates any child enumerations created before the modification.
methods.returns: an Enumeration of this node's children

methods.name: setAllowsChildren
methods.description: Determines whether or not this node is allowed to have children. If allows is false, all of this node's children are removed. Note: By default, a node allows children.

parameters.name: allows
parameters.description: true if this node is allowed to have children

methods.name: getAllowsChildren
methods.description: Returns true if this node is allowed to have children.
methods.returns: true if this node allows children, else false

methods.name: setUserObject
methods.description: Sets the user object for this node to userObject.

parameters.name: userObject
parameters.description: the Object that constitutes this node's user-specified data

methods.name: getUserObject
methods.description: Returns this node's user object.
methods.returns: the Object stored at this node by the user

methods.name: removeFromParent
methods.description: Removes the subtree rooted at this node from the tree, giving this node a null parent. Does nothing if this node is the root of its tree.

methods.name: remove
methods.description: Removes aChild from this node's child array, giving it a null parent.

parameters.name: aChild
parameters.description: a child of this node to remove

throws.description: if aChild is null or is not a child of this node

methods.name: removeAllChildren
methods.description: Removes all of this node's children, setting their parents to null. If this node has no children, this method does nothing.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Removes newChild from its parent and makes it a child of this node by adding it to the end of this node's child array.

parameters.name: newChild
parameters.description: node to add as a child of this node

throws.description: if this node does not allow children

methods.name: isNodeAncestor
methods.description: Returns true if anotherNode is an ancestor of this node -- if it is this node, this node's parent, or an ancestor of this node's parent. (Note that a node is considered an ancestor of itself.) If anotherNode is null, this method returns false. This operation is at worst O(h) where h is the distance from the root to this node.

parameters.name: anotherNode
parameters.description: node to test as an ancestor of this node

methods.returns: true if this node is a descendant of anotherNode

methods.name: isNodeDescendant
methods.description: Returns true if anotherNode is a descendant of this node -- if it is this node, one of this node's children, or a descendant of one of this node's children. Note that a node is considered a descendant of itself. If anotherNode is null, returns false. This operation is at worst O(h) where h is the distance from the root to anotherNode.

parameters.name: anotherNode
parameters.description: node to test as descendant of this node

methods.returns: true if this node is an ancestor of anotherNode

methods.name: getSharedAncestor
methods.description: Returns the nearest common ancestor to this node and aNode. Returns null, if no such ancestor exists -- if this node and aNode are in different trees or if aNode is null. A node is considered an ancestor of itself.

parameters.name: aNode
parameters.description: node to find common ancestor with

methods.returns: nearest ancestor common to this node and aNode, or null if none

methods.name: isNodeRelated
methods.description: Returns true if and only if aNode is in the same tree as this node. Returns false if aNode is null.

parameters.name: aNode
parameters.description: node to find common ancestor with

methods.returns: true if aNode is in the same tree as this node; false if aNode is null

methods.name: getDepth
methods.description: Returns the depth of the tree rooted at this node -- the longest distance from this node to a leaf. If this node has no children, returns 0. This operation is much more expensive than getLevel() because it must effectively traverse the entire tree rooted at this node.
methods.returns: the depth of the tree whose root is this node

methods.name: getLevel
methods.description: Returns the number of levels above this node -- the distance from the root to this node. If this node is the root, returns 0.
methods.returns: the number of levels above this node

methods.name: getPath
methods.description: Returns the path from the root, to get to this node. The last element in the path is this node.
methods.returns: an array of TreeNode objects giving the path, where the first element in the path is the root and the last element is this node.

methods.name: getPathToRoot
methods.signature: protected TreeNode[] getPathToRoot(TreeNode aNode, int depth)
methods.description: Builds the parents of node up to and including the root node, where the original node is the last element in the returned array. The length of the returned array gives the node's depth in the tree.

parameters.name: aNode
parameters.description: the TreeNode to get the path for

parameters.name: depth
parameters.description: an int giving the number of steps already taken towards the root (on recursive calls), used to size the returned array

methods.returns: an array of TreeNodes giving the path from the root to the specified node

methods.name: getUserObjectPath
methods.description: Returns the user object path, from the root, to get to this node. If some of the TreeNodes in the path have null user objects, the returned path will contain nulls.
methods.returns: the user object path, from the root, to get to this node

methods.name: getRoot
methods.description: Returns the root of the tree that contains this node. The root is the ancestor with a null parent.
methods.returns: the root of the tree that contains this node

methods.name: isRoot
methods.description: Returns true if this node is the root of the tree. The root is the only node in the tree with a null parent; every tree has exactly one root.
methods.returns: true if this node is the root of its tree

methods.name: getNextNode
methods.description: Returns the node that follows this node in a preorder traversal of this node's tree. Returns null if this node is the last node of the traversal. This is an inefficient way to traverse the entire tree; use an enumeration, instead.
methods.returns: the node that follows this node in a preorder traversal, or null if this node is last

methods.name: getPreviousNode
methods.description: Returns the node that precedes this node in a preorder traversal of this node's tree. Returns null if this node is the first node of the traversal -- the root of the tree. This is an inefficient way to traverse the entire tree; use an enumeration, instead.
methods.returns: the node that precedes this node in a preorder traversal, or null if this node is the first

methods.name: preorderEnumeration
methods.description: Creates and returns an enumeration that traverses the subtree rooted at this node in preorder. The first node returned by the enumeration's nextElement() method is this node. Modifying the tree by inserting, removing, or moving a node invalidates any enumerations created before the modification.
methods.returns: an enumeration for traversing the tree in preorder

methods.name: postorderEnumeration
methods.description: Creates and returns an enumeration that traverses the subtree rooted at this node in postorder. The first node returned by the enumeration's nextElement() method is the leftmost leaf. This is the same as a depth-first traversal. Modifying the tree by inserting, removing, or moving a node invalidates any enumerations created before the modification.
methods.returns: an enumeration for traversing the tree in postorder

methods.name: breadthFirstEnumeration
methods.description: Creates and returns an enumeration that traverses the subtree rooted at this node in breadth-first order. The first node returned by the enumeration's nextElement() method is this node. Modifying the tree by inserting, removing, or moving a node invalidates any enumerations created before the modification.
methods.returns: an enumeration for traversing the tree in breadth-first order

methods.name: depthFirstEnumeration
methods.description: Creates and returns an enumeration that traverses the subtree rooted at this node in depth-first order. The first node returned by the enumeration's nextElement() method is the leftmost leaf. This is the same as a postorder traversal. Modifying the tree by inserting, removing, or moving a node invalidates any enumerations created before the modification.
methods.returns: an enumeration for traversing the tree in depth-first order

methods.name: pathFromAncestorEnumeration
methods.description: Creates and returns an enumeration that follows the path from ancestor to this node. The enumeration's nextElement() method first returns ancestor, then the child of ancestor that is an ancestor of this node, and so on, and finally returns this node. Creation of the enumeration is O(m) where m is the number of nodes between this node and ancestor, inclusive. Each nextElement() message is O(1). Modifying the tree by inserting, removing, or moving a node invalidates any enumerations created before the modification.

parameters.name: ancestor
parameters.description: the node to start enumeration from

throws.description: if ancestor is not an ancestor of this node

methods.returns: an enumeration for following the path from an ancestor of this node to this one

methods.name: isNodeChild
methods.description: Returns true if aNode is a child of this node. If aNode is null, this method returns false.

parameters.name: aNode
parameters.description: the node to determinate whether it is a child

methods.returns: true if aNode is a child of this node; false if aNode is null

methods.name: getFirstChild
methods.description: Returns this node's first child. If this node has no children, throws NoSuchElementException.

throws.description: if this node has no children

methods.returns: the first child of this node

methods.name: getLastChild
methods.description: Returns this node's last child. If this node has no children, throws NoSuchElementException.

throws.description: if this node has no children

methods.returns: the last child of this node

methods.name: getChildAfter
methods.description: Returns the child in this node's child array that immediately follows aChild, which must be a child of this node. If aChild is the last child, returns null. This method performs a linear search of this node's children for aChild and is O(n) where n is the number of children; to traverse the entire array of children, use an enumeration instead.

parameters.name: aChild
parameters.description: the child node to look for next child after it

throws.description: if aChild is null or is not a child of this node

methods.returns: the child of this node that immediately follows aChild

methods.name: getChildBefore
methods.description: Returns the child in this node's child array that immediately precedes aChild, which must be a child of this node. If aChild is the first child, returns null. This method performs a linear search of this node's children for aChild and is O(n) where n is the number of children.

parameters.name: aChild
parameters.description: the child node to look for previous child before it

throws.description: if aChild is null or is not a child of this node

methods.returns: the child of this node that immediately precedes aChild

methods.name: isNodeSibling
methods.description: Returns true if anotherNode is a sibling of (has the same parent as) this node. A node is its own sibling. If anotherNode is null, returns false.

parameters.name: anotherNode
parameters.description: node to test as sibling of this node

methods.returns: true if anotherNode is a sibling of this node

methods.name: getSiblingCount
methods.description: Returns the number of siblings of this node. A node is its own sibling (if it has no parent or no siblings, this method returns 1).
methods.returns: the number of siblings of this node

methods.name: getNextSibling
methods.description: Returns the next sibling of this node in the parent's children array. Returns null if this node has no parent or is the parent's last child. This method performs a linear search that is O(n) where n is the number of children; to traverse the entire array, use the parent's child enumeration instead.
methods.returns: the sibling of this node that immediately follows this node

methods.name: getPreviousSibling
methods.description: Returns the previous sibling of this node in the parent's children array. Returns null if this node has no parent or is the parent's first child. This method performs a linear search that is O(n) where n is the number of children.
methods.returns: the sibling of this node that immediately precedes this node

methods.name: isLeaf
methods.description: Returns true if this node has no children. To distinguish between nodes that have no children and nodes that cannot have children (e.g. to distinguish files from empty directories), use this method in conjunction with getAllowsChildren
methods.returns: true if this node has no children

methods.name: getFirstLeaf
methods.description: Finds and returns the first leaf that is a descendant of this node -- either this node or its first child's first leaf. Returns this node if it is a leaf.
methods.returns: the first leaf in the subtree rooted at this node

methods.name: getLastLeaf
methods.description: Finds and returns the last leaf that is a descendant of this node -- either this node or its last child's last leaf. Returns this node if it is a leaf.
methods.returns: the last leaf in the subtree rooted at this node

methods.name: getNextLeaf
methods.description: Returns the leaf after this node or null if this node is the last leaf in the tree. In this implementation of the MutableNode interface, this operation is very inefficient. In order to determine the next node, this method first performs a linear search in the parent's child-list in order to find the current node. That implementation makes the operation suitable for short traversals from a known position. But to traverse all of the leaves in the tree, you should use depthFirstEnumeration to enumerate the nodes in the tree and use isLeaf on each node to determine which are leaves.
methods.returns: returns the next leaf past this node

methods.name: getPreviousLeaf
methods.description: Returns the leaf before this node or null if this node is the first leaf in the tree. In this implementation of the MutableNode interface, this operation is very inefficient. In order to determine the previous node, this method first performs a linear search in the parent's child-list in order to find the current node. That implementation makes the operation suitable for short traversals from a known position. But to traverse all of the leaves in the tree, you should use depthFirstEnumeration to enumerate the nodes in the tree and use isLeaf on each node to determine which are leaves.
methods.returns: returns the leaf before this node

methods.name: getLeafCount
methods.description: Returns the total number of leaves that are descendants of this node. If this node is a leaf, returns 1. This method is O(n) where n is the number of descendants of this node.
methods.returns: the number of leaves beneath this node

methods.name: toString
methods.description: Returns the result of sending toString() to this node's user object, or the empty string if the node has no user object.
methods.returns: a string representation of the object.

methods.name: clone
methods.description: Overridden to make clone public. Returns a shallow copy of this node; the new node has no parent or children and has a reference to the same user object, if any.
methods.returns: a copy of this node
